daily_summaries.1:
On Day 1 of the week, Star reported significant developments regarding the new studio space in Porto. Today, Star visited the studio located in the Bonfim district and felt excited about its potential, noting that it has a great vibe that will inspire their work. 

Star made an investment of approximately 500 euros on studio supplies, including clay, sculpting tools, and glazes, to ensure they are well-equipped from the start. In addition to setting up the studio, Star met with collaborator Ana to discuss details for a group exhibition involving four other artists. Star expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration, highlighting that each artist brings unique contributions, which will foster a fantastic dynamic for the exhibition.

Despite the busy schedule, Star feels prepared to manage both the demands of the new studio and the exhibition, believing that the energy from one project will positively influence the other. Overall, today marked a productive and optimistic start to the week for Star in their artistic endeavors.
daily_summaries.2:
On Day 1 of the week, Star reported significant developments regarding the new studio space in Porto. They visited the studio located in the Bonfim district and felt excited about its potential, noting that it has a great vibe that will inspire their work. Star made an investment of approximately 500 euros on studio supplies, including clay, sculpting tools, and glazes, to ensure they are well-equipped from the start. In addition to setting up the studio, Star met with collaborator Ana to discuss details for a group exhibition involving four other artists. Star expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration, highlighting that each artist brings unique contributions, which will foster a fantastic dynamic for the exhibition. 

On Day 2, Star attended a workshop at the Porto Art School, where they spent about three hours learning new glazing techniques. They particularly enjoyed experimenting with layering glazes and were inspired by the unique approaches of other artists. Star noted that one artist's use of color stood out to them, prompting thoughts about incorporating bolder choices into their work. This workshop bolstered Star's confidence in preparation for the upcoming exhibition, and they believe the new techniques learned will positively influence their projects in the studio. Overall, Star feels ready to take on both the demands of the new studio and the exhibition, with a productive and optimistic outlook for their artistic endeavors.
daily_summaries.3:
On Day 1 of the week, Star reported significant developments regarding the new studio space in Porto. They visited the studio located in the Bonfim district and felt excited about its potential, noting that it has a great vibe that will inspire their work. Star made an investment of approximately 500 euros on studio supplies, including clay, sculpting tools, and glazes, to ensure they are well-equipped from the start. In addition to setting up the studio, Star met with collaborator Ana to discuss details for a group exhibition involving four other artists. Star expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration, highlighting that each artist brings unique contributions, fostering a fantastic dynamic for the exhibition.

On Day 2, Star attended a workshop at the Porto Art School, where they spent about three hours learning new glazing techniques. They particularly enjoyed experimenting with layering glazes and were inspired by the unique approaches of other artists. This workshop bolstered Star's confidence in preparation for the upcoming exhibition, and they believe the new techniques learned will positively influence their projects in the studio.

On Day 3, Star worked intensely for 10 hours in the studio on new pieces to prepare for the exhibition. However, they expressed feeling overwhelmed by the amount of work left to do and the responsibility of coordinating with the four other artists. Star mentioned an argument with their partner regarding the exhibition timeline, noting they have different ideas about how quickly things should come together. They are still navigating this disagreement, aiming for a resolution while focusing on their creative process. Despite the pressure, Star is determined to remember their passion for art to push through the long hours in the studio.
daily_summaries.4:
On Day 1 of the week, Star reported significant developments regarding the new studio space in Porto. They visited the studio located in the Bonfim district and felt excited about its potential, noting that it has a great vibe that will inspire their work. Star made an investment of approximately 500 euros on studio supplies, including clay, sculpting tools, and glazes, to ensure they are well-equipped from the start. In addition to setting up the studio, Star met with collaborator Ana to discuss details for a group exhibition involving four other artists. Star expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration, highlighting that each artist brings unique contributions, fostering a fantastic dynamic for the exhibition.

On Day 2, Star attended a workshop at the Porto Art School, where they spent about three hours learning new glazing techniques. They particularly enjoyed experimenting with layering glazes and were inspired by the unique approaches of other artists. This workshop bolstered Star's confidence in preparation for the upcoming exhibition, and they believe the new techniques learned will positively influence their projects in the studio.

On Day 3, Star worked intensely for 10 hours in the studio on new pieces to prepare for the exhibition. However, they expressed feeling overwhelmed by the amount of work left to do and the responsibility of coordinating with the four other artists. Star mentioned an argument with their partner regarding the exhibition timeline, noting they have different ideas about how quickly things should come together. They are still navigating this disagreement, aiming for a resolution while focusing on their creative process. Despite the pressure, Star is determined to remember their passion for art to push through the long hours in the studio.

On Day 4, Star visited the local pottery market for inspiration, purchasing 15 different types of clay, enjoying the tactile experience and interactions with fellow artisans. They felt joyful while discussing ideas with others, which contributed to their creative energy. Star reported that progress on the group exhibition is going well, with collaborative efforts among the artists enriching their concepts and techniques, enhancing the overall project.
daily_summaries.5:
On Day 1 of the week, Star reported significant developments regarding the new studio space in Porto, located in the Bonfim district. They felt excited about its potential, noting a great vibe to inspire their work. Star made an investment of approximately 500 euros on studio supplies, including clay, sculpting tools, and glazes. In addition to setting up the studio, Star met with collaborator Ana to discuss a group exhibition involving four other artists, expressing enthusiasm about the collaboration.

On Day 2, Star attended a workshop at the Porto Art School for about three hours, learning new glazing techniques and experimenting with layering glazes. This experience boosted their confidence in preparation for the upcoming exhibition.

On Day 3, Star worked intensely for 10 hours in the studio on new pieces but felt overwhelmed by the workload and the responsibility of coordinating with the other artists. They mentioned an argument with their partner regarding the exhibition timeline, which they are still navigating.

On Day 4, Star visited the local pottery market, purchasing 15 types of clay and enjoying interactions with artisans. They reported that progress on the group exhibition is going well.

On Day 5, Star had a productive meeting with the other exhibition artists, spending about 5 hours planning the layout of the exhibition. They felt optimistic about the collaboration, believing they had created something special together and discussing innovative presentation ideas. Star credited their previous experience with their first studio opening for their confidence in this exhibition's planning, expressing an overall sense of optimism as the event approaches.
daily_summaries.6:
On Day 1 of the week, Star reported significant developments regarding the new studio space in Porto, located in the Bonfim district. They felt excited about its potential, noting a great vibe to inspire their work. Star made an investment of approximately 500 euros on studio supplies, including clay, sculpting tools, and glazes. In addition to setting up the studio, Star met with collaborator Ana to discuss a group exhibition involving four other artists, expressing enthusiasm about the collaboration.

On Day 2, Star attended a workshop at the Porto Art School for about three hours, learning new glazing techniques and experimenting with layering glazes. This experience boosted their confidence in preparation for the upcoming exhibition.

On Day 3, Star worked intensely for 10 hours in the studio on new pieces but felt overwhelmed by the workload and the responsibility of coordinating with the other artists. They mentioned an argument with their partner regarding the exhibition timeline, which they are still navigating.

On Day 4, Star visited the local pottery market, purchasing 15 types of clay and enjoying interactions with artisans. They reported that progress on the group exhibition is going well.

On Day 5, Star had a productive meeting with the other exhibition artists, spending about 5 hours planning the layout of the exhibition. They felt optimistic about the collaboration, believing they had created something special together and discussing innovative presentation ideas. Star credited their previous experience with their first studio opening for their confidence in this exhibition's planning, expressing an overall sense of optimism as the event approaches.

On Day 6, Star visited the local gallery to finalize the exhibition space for the group show, reporting that the meeting went smoothly and that they were happy with the chosen space. They invested 200 euros in promotional materials to ensure everything looks professional for the exhibition, although they felt anxious about promoting their work. Star is focusing on the excitement of sharing their art and connecting with other artists to manage this anxiety. They plan to work on the final touches of their pieces and determine the best way to display them in the upcoming exhibition.
daily_summaries.7:
On Day 1 of the week, Star reported significant developments regarding the new studio space in Porto, located in the Bonfim district. They felt excited about its potential, noting a great vibe to inspire their work. Star made an investment of approximately 500 euros on studio supplies, including clay, sculpting tools, and glazes. In addition to setting up the studio, Star met with collaborator Ana to discuss a group exhibition involving four other artists, expressing enthusiasm about the collaboration.

On Day 2, Star attended a workshop at the Porto Art School for about three hours, learning new glazing techniques and experimenting with layering glazes. This experience boosted their confidence in preparation for the upcoming exhibition.

On Day 3, Star worked intensely for 10 hours in the studio on new pieces but felt overwhelmed by the workload and the responsibility of coordinating with the other artists. They mentioned an argument with their partner regarding the exhibition timeline, which they are still navigating.

On Day 4, Star visited the local pottery market, purchasing 15 types of clay and enjoying interactions with artisans. They reported that progress on the group exhibition is going well.

On Day 5, Star had a productive meeting with the other exhibition artists, spending about 5 hours planning the layout of the exhibition. They felt optimistic about the collaboration, believing they had created something special together and discussing innovative presentation ideas. Star credited their previous experience with their first studio opening for their confidence in this exhibition's planning, expressing an overall sense of optimism as the event approaches.

On Day 6, Star visited the local gallery to finalize the exhibition space for the group show, reporting that the meeting went smoothly and that they were happy with the chosen space. They invested 200 euros in promotional materials to ensure everything looks professional for the exhibition, although they felt anxious about promoting their work. Star is focusing on the excitement of sharing their art and connecting with other artists to manage this anxiety. They plan to work on the final touches of their pieces and determine the best way to display them in the upcoming exhibition.

On Day 7, Star celebrated the end of a busy week by going out for dinner with friends at a cozy place in Porto, which they described as a wonderful way to unwind. They spent about 4 hours preparing the final pieces for the exhibition and felt proud of their accomplishments, noting that each piece reflects their dedication and effort. The most challenging aspect of preparation was managing their time effectively while coordinating with the other artists. Star expressed optimism about the new studio space, believing it will expand their creativity and open up new opportunities for collaboration with other artists in the future.
